hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Ace, 3, 4. You bet. Your opponent called, staying patient.
Turn — 5. You bet. Your opponent raised — they just connected.
River — 7. Your opponent bet again when checked to.

wrongReasons: 5 K has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 5 K.
6 5 makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
8 10 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only high card wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K 5

Your opponent called the flop with overcards, then the turn card gave them a pair. The raise on the turn — right after that card landed — is the tell: passive until they hit, then immediately aggressive.
